label: Manage a credential
description: Help users understand, control, and maintain credentials they already hold — including viewing status, responding to changes, and taking actions such as renewal or removal.
use_when: Users need to see what credentials they hold.
Users need to understand validity, expiry, or revocation status.
Users need to respond to changes that affect future service access.
do_not_use_when: The user does not yet have a credential — use get-a-credential instead.

building_blocks.notes: For notifying users when credential status changes.

steps.label: Access credentials
steps.description: User accesses credentials via wallet, account area, or assisted channel. Must clarify custody, storage location, and persistence.

steps.label: View credentials
steps.description: Show list of credentials with name, purpose, and reuse potential in plain language.

steps.label: View credential details and status
steps.description: Show what the credential proves, who issued it, current status (valid/expired/revoked), and expiry date.

steps.label: Notify of change (if applicable)
steps.description: Notify user if credential has expired, been revoked, or been replaced.

steps.label: Take action on credential (optional)
steps.description: Allow user to remove, replace, renew, or recover a credential where permitted.

condition.label: User wants to take action after notification

ux_considerations.text: Translate credential status into plain language meaning (e.g. 'You can use this', 'You need to update this').
Do not rely on users checking proactively — send notifications when status changes.
Ensure users can view key credential information when offline.

technical_notes.text: Credential status must be fetched in real-time or near-real-time, not cached without expiry.
